ddls:
CREATE TABLE "monitoring_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device or sensor that collected the data */,
  "time" REAL /* Time of data collection */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of data collection */,
  "ping" INTEGER /* Ping time */,
  "packet_loss" INTEGER /* Packet loss */,
  "stream_fps" INTEGER /* Stream FPS */,
  "used_bandwidth" INTEGER /* Used bandwidth */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution */,
  "available_bandwidth" INTEGER /* Available bandwidth */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of data */,
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the resolution */,
  "metadata" TEXT /* Additional metadata for the data point */,
  "alert_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if this data point has triggered an alert */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the alert if an alert is triggered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_monitoring_data_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_monitoring_data_resolution_id FOREIGN KEY ("resolution_id") REFERENCES resolutions ("resolution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_monitoring_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., router, switch, sensor) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data type */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value for the data type */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value for the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resolutions" (
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resolution */,
  "resolution_name" TEXT /* Name of the resolution */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resolution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, administrator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the data */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES monitoring_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high packet loss, low available bandwidth) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message about the alert */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES monitoring_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rule */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data to which the rule applies */,
  "threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold value for triggering the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert to trigger */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the rule */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the rule is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the rule was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the rule was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert that triggered the notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Detailed message of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "read_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)
